extracted.headquarters: 1010 North University Parks Drive, Waco, Texas 76707
extracted.brandBackground: Real Property Management has offered property management franchises since 2005 (through predecessor Property Management Business Solutions, LLC), was acquired by Neighborly in 2018, and restructured under a securitization transaction in 2021; as of December 31, 2025, 450 franchises operate in the U.S.
extracted.conceptRationale: Franchisees operate office-based residential and commercial property management businesses providing tenant screening, leasing, rent collection, maintenance coordination, and evictions — a home/trade services model with no food or entertainment component.
extracted.staffingRationale: Item 15 requires owner or designated manager on-site supervision; Item 1 notes 500–1,000 sq ft office with small staff, consistent with a small-crew semi-absentee model.

leadership.name: Jeffrey Pepperney
leadership.background: President of RPM SPV LLC since March 2021; previously President of Predecessor from September 2019.
leadership.whyItMatters: Top operational leader of the franchise system.

leadership.name: Michael Anthony Davis
leadership.background: CEO since November 2024; previously CEO of Mathnasium Center Licensing and Valpak Direct Marketing.
leadership.whyItMatters: Sets strategic direction for the entire Neighborly platform including RPM.

leadership.name: Timothy Sedgwick
leadership.background: VP of Operations since March 2021; previously Director of National Accounts at Predecessor since 2012.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees day-to-day franchise operations and support.

leadership.name: Malia Gelfo
leadership.background: SVP Corporate Controller since December 2024; previously CAO at Likewize Corp and VP Controller at Reece Inc.
leadership.whyItMatters: Responsible for financial reporting across the franchisor and affiliates.

leadership.name: Grayson Brown
leadership.background: EVP, General Counsel and Secretary of Neighborly since May 2018; Secretary of RPM SPV LLC since March 2021.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees all legal matters including franchise agreement compliance.

leadership.name: Bradley Stevenson
leadership.background: Chief Development Officer for Neighborly since October 2019.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads franchise sales and development strategy for RPM and all Neighborly brands.

cohorts.label: Franchises 1–3 Years Old – Annual Revenue per Unit
cohorts.basis: Average Annual Revenue per property unit managed; 81 franchises open 1–3 years, FY2025; revenue per unit not per franchise.

cohorts.label: Franchises Over 3 Years Old – Annual Revenue per Unit
cohorts.basis: Average Annual Revenue per property unit managed; 340 franchises open >3 years, FY2025; revenue per unit not per franchise.

cohorts.label: All Reporting Franchises – Annual Revenue per Unit
cohorts.basis: Average Annual Revenue per property unit managed across all 421 reporting franchises, FY2025.

cohorts.label: All Reporting Franchises – Average Units Managed (1–3 Years)
cohorts.basis: Average 101 units managed per franchise (1–3 years old); median 57 units.

cohorts.label: All Reporting Franchises – Average Units Managed (Over 3 Years)
cohorts.basis: Average 312 units managed per franchise (>3 years old); median 155 units.

cohorts.label: All Reporting Franchises – Average Units Managed (Overall)
cohorts.basis: Average 260 units managed per franchise overall; median 123 units.

item19.notes: Item 19 discloses revenue per property unit managed (not per franchise location) and average/median unit counts by franchise age cohort. No EBITDA or profitability table is disclosed. Average monthly rent per managed unit is $1,541 (median $1,496) across 420 reporting franchises. No costs or expenses are included in the Item 19 representation.

lineItems.notes: Paid in lump sum upon signing Franchise Agreement; non-refundable.
Pre-opening and early marketing spend; paid to third parties as incurred.
Estimated first 3 months rent for 500–1,000 sq ft office; paid to third parties before opening.
Low reflects applying marks to existing vehicle; high reflects vehicle purchase plus marks.
Required insurance coverage before beginning operations; paid to us and third parties.
Office equipment, inventory, signage; low assumes existing business; high assumes new start.
Franchisee responsible for travel and living expenses during training; training cost included in franchise fee.
AppFolio setup fee ($200) plus monthly fees; paid to third-party vendor before opening.
Monthly Software System fee paid via ACH upon commencement and monthly thereafter.

lineItems.category: Task Management and Lead Management Software
lineItems.notes: Monthly LeadSimple fee paid via ACH upon commencement and monthly thereafter.
State/local permits, real estate/brokerage license fees, Chamber of Commerce memberships.
Attorney, accountant, and consultant fees for business establishment.
Working capital reserve for first 12 months; covers employees, utilities, lessor, and suppliers.

flatMonthlyFees.name: Task Management and Lead Management Software (up to 110 units)
Digital Marketing Program (new offices, first 12 months)

hiddenCosts.name: BackOffice Bookkeeping Assistance Fee (per unit)
hiddenCosts.description: Required for minimum 12 months; currently $17.00 per property unit under management with $400 minimum per month.

hiddenCosts.name: Property Management Software (AppFolio) Monthly Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Mandatory third-party software; $258/month minimum (first 200 units), $1.29 per additional unit; one-time $200 setup fee.

hiddenCosts.name: Quarterly Bank Review Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Optional after HelpDesk Plus; $350 first quarter, $250 each subsequent quarter, paid to BackOffice.

hiddenCosts.name: Annual Convention/Reunion Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Mandatory annual attendance; currently $1,000 per person; $2,000 penalty for non-attendance.

hiddenCosts.name: Minimum Local Marketing Spending
hiddenCosts.description: Greater of $32,000/year or 5% of prior year Non-Maintenance Gross Sales; in addition to MAP Fee.

hiddenCosts.name: Paradox ATS (optional applicant tracking)
hiddenCosts.description: Optional third-party job applicant tracking system; currently $703/year.

hiddenCosts.name: Real Estate Brokerage License
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisee must obtain and maintain a real estate license and sponsoring broker before training; costs vary by state.

hiddenCosts.name: Transfer Fee
hiddenCosts.description: One-time $10,000 fee payable to franchisor upon sale/transfer of the Business.

hiddenCosts.name: Renewal Fee
hiddenCosts.description: One-time $3,000 fee payable upon renewal of the Franchise Agreement.

hiddenCosts.name: Late Fee (Franchise Agreement)
hiddenCosts.description: $10 per day on overdue fees from due date until paid.

hiddenCosts.name: Fine for Marketing Outside Territory
hiddenCosts.description: $500 per violation for marketing, soliciting, or performing services outside Territory without prior written consent.

hiddenCosts.name: Supplemental Training Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Currently $250 per day plus travel expenses if franchisee is required to repeat training.

hiddenCosts.name: Ongoing Training Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Currently $600–$1,500 per session (up to 2+ sessions per year, up to 4 days each).

hiddenCosts.name: Podium AI Concierge Add-On
hiddenCosts.description: Optional add-on to Software System; currently $50/month.

hiddenCosts.name: Additional RPM Intranet Users (beyond 2)
hiddenCosts.description: $7/month per additional user beyond the 2 included in the base Software System fee.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum License Fee Payments
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee must pay minimum license fees regardless of sales levels; failure may result in franchise termination and loss of investment.
operationalRisks.source: Special Risks, p. v; Item 6, p. 20

operationalRisks.title: Sales Performance Minimum Standards
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee must maintain annual Gross Sales in the top 90% of the system and a Net Promoter Score within 10 points of system average; failure can result in territory reduction or termination.
operationalRisks.source: Special Risks, p. v; Item 12, p. 56

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Liability Guarantee
operationalRisks.description: Spouse may be required to personally guarantee all financial obligations under the Franchise Agreement, placing marital assets at risk.
operationalRisks.source: Special Risks, p. v; Item 10, p. 41

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution (Texas)
operationalRisks.description: All disputes must be mediated, arbitrated, or litigated in McLennan County, Texas, which may disadvantage out-of-state franchisees.
operationalRisks.source: Special Risks, p. v; Item 17, p. 67

operationalRisks.title: Non-Exclusive Territory
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee does not receive an exclusive territory; franchisor may compete via National Accounts, alternative channels, or affiliate brands within the Territory.

operationalRisks.title: Post-Termination Non-Compete (2 Years / 25 Miles)
operationalRisks.description: For 2 years after termination, franchisee cannot operate a competitive business within the Territory or within 25 miles of its outer boundary.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Proprietary Software and BackOffice Programs
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee must use franchisor-designated software (AppFolio, LeadSimple, BackOffice) for minimum periods; fees can increase up to 30% annually.

operationalRisks.title: Real Estate Brokerage License Requirement
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee must obtain a state real estate license and sponsoring broker before attending training; licensing timelines vary by state and can delay opening.

operationalRisks.title: Pending Litigation – Restrictive Covenant Enforcement
operationalRisks.description: A pending California lawsuit (Solano Properties) challenges enforceability of post-termination restrictive covenants under California's UCL.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor Assignment Without Consent
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor may assign the Franchise Agreement to any third party without prior notice to or consent of the franchisee.

operationalRisks.title: Business Model and Fee Changes Without Consent
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor may change the Operating Manual, System standards, and fees without franchisee consent, potentially requiring additional investment.

rentDetail.source: Item 7, p. 29–30 — low estimate based on 500 sq ft, high on 1,000 sq ft; $2,250–$9,000 covers first 3 months rent; midpoint office ~750 sq ft implied

riskReasons: 3 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.
Franchisee unit economics could not be scored — no usable Item 19 (company/affiliate-owned only, or no FPR). Treat this as UNVERIFIED, not low-risk.
notes: No Item 19 middle-cohort or network-average revenue found; economics are indeterminate.

underwriting.assessment: $250,000 liquid covers the $171,822 mid-point build-out without financing, though that leaves little working-capital cushion.

insights.conceptRationale: Franchisees operate office-based residential and commercial property management businesses providing tenant screening, leasing, rent collection, maintenance coordination, and evictions — a home/trade services model with no food or entertainment component.

benchmark.label: Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Crew utilization, callback/warranty cost, and customer-acquisition cost. Low fixed overhead, but CAC and scheduling efficiency decide profitability.
benchmark.rampNote: 3–9 months to a steady job pipeline.
considerations: Budget materials/subcontractor COGS ~25–40% and field labor ~20–35%.
CAC (lead cost per booked job) is the dominant hidden line — ask Item 20 operators what they actually spend per job.
Confirm territory size, lead-generation expectations, and any required call-center/marketing fees.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–25% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

insights.staffingNote: Flagged as an lightly staffed model from the FDD, so labor is adjusted down to 10–21% — the 20–35% category band assumes a staffed venue. Confirm actual staffing with Item 20 franchisees.

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Read as lightly staffed from the FDD, which lowers the labor band applied below.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: This franchisor is losing money as it grows (a net loss of $185.84M in the most recent year), though it still has positive net worth. Worth understanding how long its runway lasts.
body: What we found (2025-12-31, audited by Ernst & Young LLP): Net loss of $185.84M on $618.76M of revenue — roughly 1.3x spent per $1 earned. Losses are widening, not shrinking. Positive net worth of $1.19B. Cash of $32.02M against $124.00M of obligations due within a year (a current ratio of 1.07).

Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. Neighborly Assetco LLC guarantees the franchisor's performance, which partly backstops this.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: elevated unit closures (28 in the last year, about 6% of the system)
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
the parent guarantees the franchisor's performance
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
